title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Language Lens: Feed Go Request Cancellation
summary: Routes mirror tables, handlers parse, query, and render directly, status codes are ad hoc, response shapes drift, and request cancellation never reaches storage. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go request cancellation increment in consistent feed-reader HTTP API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Propagate the request context through application, database, and downstream work while preventing client disconnect from corrupting durable jobs.
Return bounded stable problem details with correlation identity and field causes without leaking internals, SQL, URLs, or secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Request Cancellation · Theory Feed Go Request Cancellation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9610 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feed Go Request Cancellation contract for the supplied data.
